mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth (PAYEMS) above 150k persons signals robust labor demand and consumer income expansion, which drives equity valuations higher through increased corporate earnings expectations over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:2012-04-01 payroll level 132989k persons indicates ongoing post-crisis recovery
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with positive labor momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following months with PAYEMS growth > 150k do not exceed baseline returns by at least 50 basis points (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate remaining below 0.25% signals an extended zero-lower-bound regime that sustains low discount rates and encourages equity risk-taking, producing positive 60-day forward returns as portfolio rebalancing toward equities persists.
evidence_refs: fact:2012-04-01 FEDFUNDS at 0.14% confirms ZLB regime
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint expansion in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals broad-based consumer and construction demand that lifts corporate revenues and equity valuations within 5 trading days as forward-looking market participants price in accelerating economic activity.
evidence_refs: fact:2012-04-01 RSAFS 408039M and 2012-03-01 HOUST 654k units show demand recovery
regime_hint: early-cycle expansion with synchronized demand signals
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns following joint RSAFS and HOUST growth do not exceed single-series baseline by at least 30 basis points.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining month-over-month signals supply-side weakness and potential margin compression, which depresses S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors anticipate lower corporate profitability.
evidence_refs: fact:2012-03-01 INDPRO 96.5685 index level reflects post-crisis production recovery
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following INDPRO declines > -0.5% are positive in > 60% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 8.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative 20-day forward returns as equity investors reprice earnings downward.
evidence_refs: fact:2012-04-01 UNRATE at 8.1% indicates elevated unemployment post-crisis
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of months when UNRATE > 8.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation (CPIAUCSL) remaining stable below 2.5% combined with accommodative FEDFUNDS below 0.5% signals monetary policy support for equity valuations, producing positive 60-day forward returns as the central bank maintains stimulus.
evidence_refs: fact:2012-04-01 CPIAUCSL 229.177 and FEDFUNDS 0.14% confirm low inflation and accommodation
failure_condition: Rank IC of the joint condition < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns fail to exceed single-series baseline by at least 40 basis points.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerating quarter-over-quarter signals strengthening aggregate demand that supports equity earnings growth, producing positive 60-day forward returns as investors revise earnings forecasts upward.
evidence_refs: fact:2012-01-01 GDPC1 13502.4B reflects post-crisis recovery trajectory
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2.0% annualized are negative in > 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth combined with rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals synchronized supply and labor expansion that sustains profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive 5-day forward returns as the market recognizes productivity gains.
evidence_refs: fact:2012-03-01 INDPRO 96.5685 and 2012-04-01 PAYEMS 132989k show coordinated recovery
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns following joint INDPRO and PAYEMS growth do not exceed single-series baseline by at least 25 basis points.
